evidences.title: ET to UTC Converter - Time.now
evidences.content: #### What is ET to UTC Conversion? ET to UTC conversion helps you translate time between Eastern Time and Coordinated Universal Time. ET encompasses both EST (UTC-5) and EDT (UTC-4) used on the U.S. East Coast. UTC is the global time standard that never changes and serves as the reference for all world timezones. The time difference varies between 4-5 hours. #### Time Zone Information Eastern Time (ET): Generic term encompassing EST (winter) and EDT (summer) in New York, Toronto, Montreal, and Miami. Automatically switches between UTC-5 and UTC-4. Coordinated Universal Time (UTC): The global time standard that never changes. Successor to GMT and used worldwide as the reference for all timezones. Also known as "Zulu Time" in aviation. [...] EDT 9:00 AM → UTC 1:00 PM EDT 5:00 PM → UTC 9:00 PM 4-hour difference during summer International Coordination EST Midnight → UTC 5:00 AM EDT Midnight → UTC 4:00 AM UTC used for global scheduling Technical Applications Server logs → UTC timestamps API calls → UTC standards UTC preferred for technical systems #### Conversion Tips & Best Practices • Add 5 hours to EST (winter) or 4 hours to EDT (summer) to get UTC • UTC is the preferred standard...

evidences.content: | Converting EDT to UTC This time zone converter lets you visually and very quickly convert EDT to UTC and vice-versa. Simply mouse over the colored hour-tiles and glance at the hours selected by the column... and done! EDT stands for Eastern Daylight Time. UTC is known as Universal Time. UTC is 4 hours ahead of EDT. evidences.title: Extract Historical Data from Binance Using Python | Medium
evidences.content: from binance.client import Clientimport pandas as pdimport datetime# Binance API credentialsapi_key = 'your_api_key'api_secret = 'your_api_secret'client = Client(api_key, api_secret)# Define the symbol for BTC/USDT pairsymbol = 'BTCUSDT'# Define custom start and end timestart_time = datetime.datetime(2024, 3, 15, 0, 0, 0)end_time = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 15, 0, 0, 0)klines = client.get_historical_klines(symbol=symbol, interval=Client.KLINE_INTERVAL_1MINUTE, start_str=str(start_time), end_str=str(end_time))# Convert the data into a pandas dataframe for easier manipulationdf_M = pd.DataFrame(klines, columns=['Open Time', 'Open', 'High', 'Low', 'Close', 'Volume', 'Close Time', 'Quote Asset Volume', 'Number of Trades', 'Taker Buy Base Asset Volume', 'Taker Buy Quote Asset Volume', [...] ``` symbol = 'BTCUSDT' 'BTCUSDT' ``` We can set a custom start and end time for our function by parsing the date to date time format. ``` # format (year, month, day, hour, minute, second)start_time = datetime.datetime(2024, 3, 15, 0, 0, 0)end_time = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 15, 0, 0, 0) # format (year, month, day, hour, minute, second) 2024 3 15 0 0 0 2024 6 15 0 0 0 ``` `datetime.datetime`: Cr...

evidences.title: EST to UTC - Online Converter
evidences.content: Based International Air Transport Association (IATA) timezone codes. Time zones use hours and minutes offset from Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), most a whole number of hours, a few are offset by 30 or 45 minutes. Eastern Standard Time (EST) is UTC-5:00, and Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) is UTC-4:00, this time zone is called Eastern Time Zone (ET) in the United States, parts of eastern Canada, Mexico, Panama and the Caribbean Islands. Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) is the primary time standard now, time zones around the world are expressed using offsets from UTC, UTC offset is the difference in hours and minutes from UTC, a time zone can be determined by adding or subtracting the number of UTC offset. evidences.title: Binance Historical Tick Data (1 second) for a specific date range and ...
evidences.content: Optimus Prime's user avatar ## 1 Answer 1 Binance provides some of its historical trading data on the `binance.vision` domain. Specifically, the 1 second klines for BTCUSDT pair can be found here: `binance.vision` Here's a sample bash script that downloads and unpacks the raw data for the whole month: `#!/bin/bash for i in $(seq -f "%02g" 1 31) # zero-padded days 01, 02, 03, ... 31 do # month of july 2023, day number is the `i` variable archiveName="BTCUSDT-1s-2023-07-${i}.zip" # download the .zip, extract it (saves .csv), and remove the .zip wget " && unzip "./${archiveName}" && rm "./${archiveName}" done` As mentioned in their GitHub readme, each of the the `.csv` lines contains the following field values: `.csv`
